metadata.objective: Restructure dev-sandbox to clearly separate 3 companies (marceau-solutions, swflorida-hvac, square-foot-shipping) with dedicated folders for each company's projects, documentation, and assets to eliminate confusion when working on multiple companies simultaneously

stories.title: Design optimal folder structure for 3-company organization
stories.description: Analyze current dev-sandbox structure and design new organization that groups all company-specific assets (projects, docs, templates, output) under company folders while maintaining shared/global resources in root. Create migration plan.
acceptance_criteria: Proposed structure clearly separates marceau-solutions, swflorida-hvac, square-foot-shipping
Each company folder contains: projects/, docs/, output/, templates/
Shared resources (execution/, methods/, global docs) stay in root
Git repository structure preserved (no nested .git)
All existing files mapped to new locations (migration plan)
Symlinks or references documented for cross-company dependencies

current_structure.projects/: Mixed - ai-customer-service (voice AI for all 3), lead-scraper (all 3), social-media-automation (all 3)

proposed_structure.NOTE: PRESERVE DOE ARCHITECTURE - dev-sandbox is DEVELOPMENT workspace, deploy to skills for production

projects/.NOTE: Keep projects/ at root - this is the DEVELOPMENT workspace per DOE

shared-multi-tenant/.lead-scraper/: Used by all 3 businesses with business_id separation
shared-multi-tenant/.social-media-automation/: Used by all 3 with business separation

marceau-solutions/.NOTE: Company-specific projects that ONLY marceau uses

swflorida-hvac/.NOTE: Company-specific projects that ONLY HVAC uses

square-foot-shipping/.NOTE: Company-specific projects that ONLY shipping uses

docs/.NOTE: Add company-specific subdirs for business docs
docs/.global/: Architecture, SOPs, deployment guides (stays at root)

output/.NOTE: Already separated by business_id in lead-scraper, just add company-specific outputs

stories.title: Categorize existing projects by company ownership
stories.description: Audit all projects in dev-sandbox and categorize as: company-specific (1 owner), shared-multi-tenant (used by all 3), or global-utility. Create mapping for where each should live in new structure.
acceptance_criteria: All projects categorized with ownership tags
Company-specific: Move to companies/{company}/projects/
Shared multi-tenant: Move to shared/projects/ with business_id separation
Global utilities: Stay in execution/
Documentation categorized (company-specific vs global)
Output files categorized (split by business_id)
Templates categorized (company-specific vs shared)

marceau-solutions: None yet - all current projects are shared
swflorida-hvac: None yet - all current projects are shared
square-foot-shipping: None yet - all current projects are shared

stories.title: Create new folder structure and migration script
stories.description: Build new companies/ and shared/ folder structure. Create automated migration script that moves files to new locations, updates imports, creates symlinks where needed, and preserves git history.
acceptance_criteria: companies/marceau-solutions/, companies/swflorida-hvac/, companies/square-foot-shipping/ created
shared/projects/, shared/execution/, shared/methods/, shared/docs/ created
Migration script: python migrate_to_company_structure.py --dry-run (shows what would move)
Migration script: python migrate_to_company_structure.py --execute (actually moves files)
Script updates relative imports (from projects/x to shared/projects/x or companies/y/projects/x)
Script creates README.md in each company folder explaining structure
Git history preserved (no force pushes)
Rollback script available in case of issues
stories.notes: CHECKPOINT: Review migration plan and new structure before executing file moves

stories.title: Migrate company-specific documentation and output
stories.description: Split documentation and output files by company. Move company-specific docs to companies/{company}/docs/, split output files by business_id, organize templates by company.
acceptance_criteria: companies/marceau-solutions/docs/ contains: AI automation docs, market analysis, strategy docs
companies/swflorida-hvac/docs/ contains: HVAC business plan, social media ready docs
companies/square-foot-shipping/docs/ contains: Logistics business docs
Output files split: companies/marceau-solutions/output/lead_intake_marceau.json
Templates organized: companies/marceau-solutions/templates/sms/
Shared docs stay in shared/docs/ (architecture, SOPs, global guides)
Cross-references updated (links in docs point to new locations)

hvac_specific: Future: HVAC business plan, service pricing, social media ready docs

stories.title: Update all scripts, imports, and documentation references
stories.description: Update all Python imports, file paths, and documentation links to reflect new folder structure. Test that all projects still work after migration. Update CLAUDE.md with new structure guide.
acceptance_criteria: All Python imports updated (from projects/x to shared/projects/x or companies/y/projects/x)
All file path references updated in scripts
deploy_to_skills.py updated with new paths
CLAUDE.md updated with new folder structure documentation
All projects tested: python -m shared.projects.lead-scraper.src.lead_intake stats (works)
Git status clean (no broken imports or missing files)
FOLDER_STRUCTURE.md created explaining new organization
Quick reference commands updated for new paths
files_to_modify: All project imports (automated via migration script)
testing_checklist: Lead scraper works for all 3 businesses
Social media automation works for all 3
Voice AI tracking works for all 3
All CLI commands work with new paths

git_safety.rule_1: NO nested .git repositories - maintain single root .git
git_safety.rule_2: Use git mv for file moves (preserves history)
git_safety.rule_3: Create migration as single atomic commit

symlink_strategy.shared_execution: All companies reference shared/execution/ (no symlinks needed)
symlink_strategy.cross_company_refs: Document in README, avoid symlinks where possible

success_metrics.clarity: Developer can instantly know which folder to access for each company
success_metrics.isolation: Company-specific work doesn't pollute other companies' folders
success_metrics.shared_efficiency: Multi-tenant projects (lead-scraper, social) work seamlessly for all 3
success_metrics.git_safety: Single .git repo, no nested repos, clean history
success_metrics.backward_compatibility: All existing commands/workflows work with minimal changes
